title: What to know about viral hepatitis before using medicinal herbs
intro: Several herbal teas exhibit potent antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, providing relief for individuals with viral hepatitis. Milk thistle tea, known for its active compound silymarin, protects the liver by promoting cell regeneration and limiting tissue damage. The soothing properties of licorice root tea help reduce inflammation and strengthen the immune system, inhibiting viral replication. Lastly, turmeric tea, rich in curcumin, demonstrates impressive antioxidant capabilities, aiding in detoxification and reducing oxidative stress on the liver. Consuming these herbal teas offers a natural, therapeutic approach to managing viral hepatitis and supporting liver health.
definition: Viral hepatitis is a serious inflammation of the liver caused by specific viruses. These viruses, named HAV, HBV, HCV, HDV, and HEV, can lead to severe health issues such as liver damage, cirrhosis, and even liver cancer. This disease affects millions of people worldwide and poses a significant threat to public health. Ignoring viral hepatitis can result in life-altering complications, making it crucial to prioritize preventive measures, timely diagnosis, and appropriate medical intervention for your overall well-being.
